title: Meter / gauge — placement of the bounded-scalar readout (role=meter)
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #1410 (surfaced by the ARIA-APG lens #1400): a meter is a static quantitative readout within a KNOWN RANGE (disk usage, score, capacity, password strength) — role=meter / native <meter> — distinct from a progress indicator (task completion over time, <progress>) and from status-indicator (discrete state). WE has status-indicator and loader but NO bounded-scalar-value readout. Native grounding: <meter> = a scalar measurement within a known range — value, min (default 0), max (default 1.0), low/high (sub-range bounds), optimum (preferred point), where low/high/optimum derive three semantic value-zones (optimum/suboptimal/even-less-good, exposed via ::-webkit-meter-* pseudo-elements); <progress> = value + max only, omitting value makes it indeterminate, NO zones/min/optimum because 'how done is this task' has no good/bad region. ARIA meter role: required aria-valuenow/min/max, optional aria-valuetext (human string), requires a meaningful max; ARIA progressbar role: aria-valuenow OMITTED when indeterminate, no zones. Load-bearing line quoted from the APG meter pattern: 'The meter should not be used to indicate progress … use the progressbar role instead' — ARIA defines these as two SEPARATE roles by design. Finding 1: meter is a bounded measurement, progress is task-over-time, and both ARIA and Carbon keep them distinct (Carbon ProgressBar = percent-complete of a long-running process; Carbon Meter/Gauge live in the data-visualization family — two homes deliberately). Finding 2: gauge = RADIAL PRESENTATION of the same value/min/max contract, not a different contract — MUI X Gauge takes value/valueMin/valueMax (same scalar-in-a-range as <meter>, rendered as an arc; open issues for gauge segments = radial low/high zones, and a linear gauge, confirm one model many presentations); Ant Progress type=dashboard/circle renders gauges as a presentation knob (though Ant over-folds via 'Progress'). Finding 3: folding meter into a progress/state component is a known DEFECT — Chakra has Progress but no Meter, its Progress applies role=progressbar, and a standing issue ('Allow using Progress as a meter') exists precisely because users want a meter but get the wrong ARIA role; rating (Shoelace sl-rating '0-5 by default, change with max') and password-strength are bounded-scalar readouts in disguise (one model, many presentations); Material/Spectrum/Fluent ship only progress and have NO meter (a market gap). WE-tree: status-indicator intent dimensions are tone (neutral|info|progress|positive|caution|critical), shape (badge|pill|dot|text), affordance (display-only|actionable) — 'the canonical display of a domain entity's LIFECYCLE STATE', the visual member of the Web Lifecycle protocol; it models DISCRETE enumerated states, NO value/min/max/low/high/optimum anywhere; status-indicator block renderStatusIndicator projects a lifecycle state onto a semantic status token (role=status), discrete; loader intent's progress dimension (determinate|indeterminate) is explicitly a progress bar reflecting transfer over time (<progress>/role=progressbar territory), NOT a standing measurement; grep for meter/gauge/rating across intents+blocks = zero hits in any value/range sense. Explicit verification: status-indicator does NOT cover a bounded scalar (it is a discrete-state enum); the unowned residual = role=meter/<meter>: a static quantitative readout within a known range, with semantic value-zones — neither loader (task-over-time) nor status-indicator (discrete state) types it. Recommendation: (Fork 1, ~90%) its OWN tiny meter intent (+ FUI block) adopting <meter>/role=meter vocabulary verbatim (value/min/max/low/high/optimum/valuetext) — folding into status-indicator is BROKEN (discrete-state enum mis-types a continuous bounded scalar, the exact Chakra defect); extending loader excluded (task-over-time; ARIA separates the roles). (Fork 2, ~75%) FILE a scoped progress sibling decision — WE already partly owns determinate task progress as loader's progress dimension, so the sibling asks whether <progress>/role=progressbar needs a first-class home or stays a loader dimension (default lean: covered by loader, ~60%); folding meter+progress into one 'readout' standard is BROKEN (ARIA defines meter and progressbar as distinct incompatible roles). Supported by default (not forks): gauge = radial PRESENTATION of the meter contract (a presentation dimension value, never a separate intent); intent + FUI block coexist; rating/password-strength = specialized meter presentations (consumers). Seams: status-indicator (discrete enum) vs meter (continuous bounded number); loader (task-over-time, may be indeterminate) vs meter (standing measurement, always determinate, has zones); a future progress<->meter boundary = the ARIA meter/progressbar split; meter is read-only display vs a range INPUT (editable form control). Report: reports/2026-06-21-bounded-scalar-readout-meter.md.
